11	/-!
12	# Ext groups in abelian categories
13	
14	Let `C` be an abelian category (with `C : Type u` and `Category.{v} C`).
15	In this file, we introduce the assumption `HasExt.{w} C` which asserts
16	that morphisms between single complexes in arbitrary degrees in
17	the derived category of `C` are `w`-small. Under this assumption,
18	we define `Ext.{w} X Y n : Type w` as shrunk versions of suitable
19	types of morphisms in the derived category. In particular, when `C` has
20	enough projectives or enough injectives, the property `HasExt.{v} C`
21	shall hold.
22	
23	Note: in certain situations, `w := v` shall be the preferred
24	choice of universe (e.g. if `C := ModuleCat.{v} R` with `R : Type v`).
25	However, in the development of the API for Ext-groups, it is important
26	to keep a larger degree of generality for universes, as `w < v`
27	may happen in certain situations. Indeed, if `X : Scheme.{u}`,
28	then the underlying category of the étale site of `X` shall be a large
29	category. However, the category `Sheaf X.Etale AddCommGrpCat.{u}`
30	shall have good properties (because there is a small category of affine
31	schemes with the same category of sheaves), and even though the type of
32	morphisms in `Sheaf X.Etale AddCommGrpCat.{u}` shall be
33	in `Type (u + 1)`, these types are going to be `u`-small.
34	Then, for `C := Sheaf X.etale AddCommGrpCat.{u}`, we will have
35	`Category.{u + 1} C`, but `HasExt.{u} C` will hold
36	(as `C` has enough injectives). Then, the `Ext` groups between étale
37	sheaves over `X` shall be in `Type u`.
38	
39	-/
40	
41	@[expose] public section
42	
43	assert_not_exists TwoSidedIdeal
44	
45	universe w'' w' w v u
46	
47	namespace CategoryTheory
48	
49	variable (C : Type u) [Category.{v} C] [Abelian C]
50	
51	open Localization Limits ZeroObject DerivedCategory Pretriangulated
52	
53	/-- The property that morphisms between single complexes in arbitrary degrees are `w`-small
54	in the derived category. -/
55	abbrev HasExt : Prop :=
56	  ∀ (X Y : C), HasSmallLocalizedShiftedHom.{w} (HomologicalComplex.quasiIso C (ComplexShape.up ℤ)) ℤ
57	    ((CochainComplex.singleFunctor C 0).obj X) ((CochainComplex.singleFunctor C 0).obj Y)
58	
59	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
60	lemma hasExt_iff [HasDerivedCategory.{w'} C] :
61	    HasExt.{w} C ↔ ∀ (X Y : C) (n : ℤ) (_ : 0 ≤ n), Small.{w}
62	      ((singleFunctor C 0).obj X ⟶
63	        (((singleFunctor C 0).obj Y)⟦n⟧)) := by
64	  dsimp [HasExt]
65	  simp only [hasSmallLocalizedShiftedHom_iff _ _ Q]
66	  constructor
67	  · intro h X Y n hn
68	    exact (small_congr ((shiftFunctorZero _ ℤ).app
69	      ((singleFunctor C 0).obj X)).homFromEquiv).1 (h X Y 0 n)
70	  · intro h X Y a b
71	    obtain hab | hab := le_or_gt a b
72	    · refine (small_congr ?_).1 (h X Y (b - a) (by simpa))
73	      exact (Functor.FullyFaithful.ofFullyFaithful
74	        (shiftFunctor _ a)).homEquiv.trans
75	        ((shiftFunctorAdd' _ _ _ _ (Int.sub_add_cancel b a)).symm.app _).homToEquiv
76	    · suffices Subsingleton ((Q.obj ((CochainComplex.singleFunctor C 0).obj X))⟦a⟧ ⟶
77	          (Q.obj ((CochainComplex.singleFunctor C 0).obj Y))⟦b⟧) from inferInstance
78	      constructor
79	      intro x y
80	      rw [← cancel_mono ((Q.commShiftIso b).inv.app _),
81	        ← cancel_epi ((Q.commShiftIso a).hom.app _)]
82	      have : (((CochainComplex.singleFunctor C 0).obj X)⟦a⟧).IsStrictlyLE (-a) :=
83	        CochainComplex.isStrictlyLE_shift _ 0 _ _ (by lia)
84	      have : (((CochainComplex.singleFunctor C 0).obj Y)⟦b⟧).IsStrictlyGE (-b) :=
85	        CochainComplex.isStrictlyGE_shift _ 0 _ _ (by lia)
86	      apply (subsingleton_hom_of_isStrictlyLE_of_isStrictlyGE _ _ (-a) (-b) (by
87	        lia)).elim
88	
89	lemma hasExt_of_hasDerivedCategory [HasDerivedCategory.{w} C] : HasExt.{w} C := by
90	  rw [hasExt_iff.{w}]
91	  infer_instance
92	
93	lemma HasExt.standard : HasExt.{max u v} C := by
94	  letI := HasDerivedCategory.standard
95	  exact hasExt_of_hasDerivedCategory _
96	
97	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
98	instance [HasExt.{w} C] (X Y : C) (a b : ℤ) [HasDerivedCategory.{w'} C] :
99	    Small.{w} ((singleFunctor C a).obj X ⟶ (singleFunctor C b).obj Y) := by
100	  have (a b : ℤ) :
101	      Small.{w} (((singleFunctor C 0).obj X)⟦a⟧ ⟶ ((singleFunctor C 0).obj Y)⟦b⟧) :=
102	    (hasSmallLocalizedShiftedHom_iff.{w}
103	      (W := (HomologicalComplex.quasiIso C (ComplexShape.up ℤ))) (M := ℤ)
104	      (X := (CochainComplex.singleFunctor C 0).obj X)
105	      (Y := (CochainComplex.singleFunctor C 0).obj Y) Q).1 inferInstance a b
106	  exact small_of_injective
107	    (β := ((singleFunctor C 0).obj X)⟦-a⟧ ⟶ ((singleFunctor C 0).obj Y)⟦-b⟧)
108	    (f := fun φ ↦
109	      ((singleFunctors C).shiftIso (-a) a 0 (by simp)).hom.app X ≫ φ ≫
110	        ((singleFunctors C).shiftIso (-b) b 0 (by simp)).inv.app Y)
111	    (fun φ₁ φ₂ h ↦ by simpa using h)

119	/-- An Ext-group in an abelian category `C`, defined as a `Type w` when `[HasExt.{w} C]`. -/
120	def Ext (X Y : C) (n : ℕ) : Type w :=
121	  SmallShiftedHom.{w} (HomologicalComplex.quasiIso C (ComplexShape.up ℤ))
122	    ((CochainComplex.singleFunctor C 0).obj X)
123	    ((CochainComplex.singleFunctor C 0).obj Y) (n : ℤ)
124	
125	namespace Ext
126	
127	variable {X Y Z T : C}
128	
129	/-- The composition of `Ext`. -/
130	noncomputable def comp {a b : ℕ} (α : Ext X Y a) (β : Ext Y Z b) {c : ℕ} (h : a + b = c) :
131	    Ext X Z c :=
132	  SmallShiftedHom.comp α β (by lia)
133	
134	lemma comp_assoc {a₁ a₂ a₃ a₁₂ a₂₃ a : ℕ} (α : Ext X Y a₁) (β : Ext Y Z a₂) (γ : Ext Z T a₃)
135	    (h₁₂ : a₁ + a₂ = a₁₂) (h₂₃ : a₂ + a₃ = a₂₃) (h : a₁ + a₂ + a₃ = a) :
136	    (α.comp β h₁₂).comp γ (show a₁₂ + a₃ = a by lia) =
137	      α.comp (β.comp γ h₂₃) (by lia) :=
138	  SmallShiftedHom.comp_assoc _ _ _ _ _ _ (by lia)
139	
140	@[simp]
141	lemma comp_assoc_of_second_deg_zero
142	    {a₁ a₃ a₁₃ : ℕ} (α : Ext X Y a₁) (β : Ext Y Z 0) (γ : Ext Z T a₃)
143	    (h₁₃ : a₁ + a₃ = a₁₃) :
144	    (α.comp β (add_zero _)).comp γ h₁₃ = α.comp (β.comp γ (zero_add _)) h₁₃ := by
145	  apply comp_assoc
146	  lia
147	
148	@[simp]
149	lemma comp_assoc_of_third_deg_zero
150	    {a₁ a₂ a₁₂ : ℕ} (α : Ext X Y a₁) (β : Ext Y Z a₂) (γ : Ext Z T 0)
151	    (h₁₂ : a₁ + a₂ = a₁₂) :
152	    (α.comp β h₁₂).comp γ (add_zero _) = α.comp (β.comp γ (add_zero _)) h₁₂ := by
153	  apply comp_assoc
154	  lia

158	variable [HasDerivedCategory.{w'} C]
159	
160	/-- When an instance of `[HasDerivedCategory.{w'} C]` is available, this is the bijection
161	between `Ext.{w} X Y n` and a type of morphisms in the derived category. -/
162	noncomputable def homEquiv {n : ℕ} :
163	    Ext.{w} X Y n ≃ ShiftedHom ((singleFunctor C 0).obj X)
164	      ((singleFunctor C 0).obj Y) (n : ℤ) :=
165	  SmallShiftedHom.equiv (HomologicalComplex.quasiIso C (ComplexShape.up ℤ)) Q
166	
167	/-- The morphism in the derived category which corresponds to an element in `Ext X Y a`. -/
168	noncomputable abbrev hom {a : ℕ} (α : Ext X Y a) :
169	    ShiftedHom ((singleFunctor C 0).obj X) ((singleFunctor C 0).obj Y) (a : ℤ) :=
170	  homEquiv α
171	
172	@[simp]
173	lemma comp_hom {a b : ℕ} (α : Ext X Y a) (β : Ext Y Z b) {c : ℕ} (h : a + b = c) :
174	    (α.comp β h).hom = α.hom.comp β.hom (by lia) := by
175	  apply SmallShiftedHom.equiv_comp
176	
177	@[ext]
178	lemma ext {n : ℕ} {α β : Ext X Y n} (h : α.hom = β.hom) : α = β :=
179	  homEquiv.injective h
180	
181	end

183	/-- The canonical map `(X ⟶ Y) → Ext X Y 0`. -/
184	noncomputable def mk₀ (f : X ⟶ Y) : Ext X Y 0 := SmallShiftedHom.mk₀ _ _ (by simp)
185	  ((CochainComplex.singleFunctor C 0).map f)
186	
187	@[simp]
188	lemma mk₀_hom [HasDerivedCategory.{w'} C] (f : X ⟶ Y) :
189	    (mk₀ f).hom = ShiftedHom.mk₀ _ (by simp) ((singleFunctor C 0).map f) := by
190	  apply SmallShiftedHom.equiv_mk₀
191	
192	@[simp]
193	lemma mk₀_comp_mk₀ (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) :
194	    (mk₀ f).comp (mk₀ g) (zero_add 0) = mk₀ (f ≫ g) := by
195	  letI := HasDerivedCategory.standard C; ext; simp
196	
197	@[simp]
198	lemma mk₀_comp_mk₀_assoc (f : X ⟶ Y) (g : Y ⟶ Z) {n : ℕ} (α : Ext Z T n) :
199	    (mk₀ f).comp ((mk₀ g).comp α (zero_add n)) (zero_add n) =
200	      (mk₀ (f ≫ g)).comp α (zero_add n) := by
201	  rw [← mk₀_comp_mk₀, comp_assoc]
202	  lia

205	variable (X Y) in
206	lemma mk₀_bijective : Function.Bijective (mk₀ (X := X) (Y := Y)) := by
207	  letI := HasDerivedCategory.standard C
208	  have h : (singleFunctor C 0).FullyFaithful := Functor.FullyFaithful.ofFullyFaithful _
209	  let e : (X ⟶ Y) ≃ Ext X Y 0 :=
210	    (h.homEquiv.trans (ShiftedHom.homEquiv _ (by simp))).trans homEquiv.symm
211	  have he : e.toFun = mk₀ := by
212	    ext f : 1
213	    dsimp [e]
214	    apply homEquiv.injective
215	    apply (Equiv.apply_symm_apply _ _).trans
216	    symm
217	    apply SmallShiftedHom.equiv_mk₀
218	  rw [← he]
219	  exact e.bijective

221	/-- The bijection `Ext X Y 0 ≃ (X ⟶ Y)`. -/
222	@[simps! symm_apply]

224	  (Equiv.ofBijective _ (mk₀_bijective X Y)).symm

227	lemma mk₀_homEquiv₀_apply (f : Ext X Y 0) :
228	    mk₀ (homEquiv₀ f) = f :=
229	  homEquiv₀.left_inv f

233	/-! The abelian group structure on `Ext X Y n` is defined by transporting the
234	abelian group structure on the constructed derived category
235	(given by `HasDerivedCategory.standard`). This constructed derived category
236	is used in order to obtain most of the compatibilities satisfied by
237	this abelian group structure. It is then shown that the bijection
238	`homEquiv` between `Ext X Y n` and Hom-types in the derived category
239	can be promoted to an additive equivalence for any `[HasDerivedCategory C]` instance. -/
240	
241	noncomputable instance : AddCommGroup (Ext X Y n) :=
242	  letI := HasDerivedCategory.standard C
243	  homEquiv.addCommGroup
244	
245	/-- The map from `Ext X Y n` to a `ShiftedHom` type in the *constructed* derived
246	category given by `HasDerivedCategory.standard`: this definition is introduced
247	only in order to prove properties of the abelian group structure on `Ext`-groups.
248	Do not use this definition: use the more general `hom` instead. -/
249	noncomputable abbrev hom' (α : Ext X Y n) :
250	    letI := HasDerivedCategory.standard C
251	    ShiftedHom ((singleFunctor C 0).obj X) ((singleFunctor C 0).obj Y) (n : ℤ) :=
252	  letI := HasDerivedCategory.standard C
253	  α.hom
254	
255	private lemma add_hom' (α β : Ext X Y n) : (α + β).hom' = α.hom' + β.hom' :=
256	  letI := HasDerivedCategory.standard C
257	  homEquiv.symm.injective (Equiv.symm_apply_apply _ _)
258	
259	private lemma neg_hom' (α : Ext X Y n) : (-α).hom' = -α.hom' :=
260	  letI := HasDerivedCategory.standard C
261	  homEquiv.symm.injective (Equiv.symm_apply_apply _ _)

265	  letI := HasDerivedCategory.standard C

340	attribute [local instance] preservesBinaryBiproducts_of_preservesBiproducts in
341	lemma biprod_ext {X₁ X₂ : C} {α β : Ext (X₁ ⊞ X₂) Y n}
342	    (h₁ : (mk₀ biprod.inl).comp α (zero_add n) = (mk₀ biprod.inl).comp β (zero_add n))
343	    (h₂ : (mk₀ biprod.inr).comp α (zero_add n) = (mk₀ biprod.inr).comp β (zero_add n)) :
344	    α = β := by
345	  letI := HasDerivedCategory.standard C
346	  rw [Ext.ext_iff] at h₁ h₂ ⊢
347	  simp only [comp_hom, mk₀_hom, ShiftedHom.mk₀_comp] at h₁ h₂
348	  apply BinaryCofan.IsColimit.hom_ext
349	    (isBinaryBilimitOfPreserves (singleFunctor C 0)
350	      (BinaryBiproduct.isBilimit X₁ X₂)).isColimit
351	  all_goals assumption

353	variable [HasDerivedCategory.{w'} C]

355	variable (X Y n) in
356	@[simp]
357	lemma zero_hom : (0 : Ext X Y n).hom = 0 := by
358	  let β : Ext 0 Y n := 0
359	  have hβ : β.hom = 0 := by apply (Functor.map_isZero _ (isZero_zero C)).eq_of_src
360	  have : (0 : Ext X Y n) = (0 : Ext X 0 0).comp β (zero_add n) := by simp [β]
361	  rw [this, comp_hom, hβ, ShiftedHom.comp_zero]
362	
363	@[simp]
364	lemma add_hom (α β : Ext X Y n) : (α + β).hom = α.hom + β.hom := by
365	  let α' : Ext (X ⊞ X) Y n := (mk₀ biprod.fst).comp α (zero_add n)
366	  let β' : Ext (X ⊞ X) Y n := (mk₀ biprod.snd).comp β (zero_add n)
367	  have eq₁ : α + β = (mk₀ (biprod.lift (𝟙 X) (𝟙 X))).comp (α' + β') (zero_add n) := by
368	    simp [α', β']
369	  have eq₂ : α' + β' = homEquiv.symm (α'.hom + β'.hom) := by

477	  toFun e i := (Ext.mk₀ (c.ι i)).comp e (zero_add n)

8	/-!
9	# Sheaves of `k`-modules: sheafification, Ext, and the structure sheaf
10	
11	Phase A step 5 (per `STRATEGY.md`):
12	
13	* **Prerequisites** (iter-005, closed): the `HasSheafify` and `HasExt` instances
14	  on the topology of opens of an arbitrary topological space, valued in
15	  `ModuleCat k`. The `Linear k` enrichment on the resulting sheaf category is
16	  auto-inferable from Mathlib and therefore needs no scaffold here; together
17	  with `CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.instModule` it gives the path to a
18	  `Module k` structure on `Ext` groups.
19	* **Main** (iter-006, scaffolded here): the structure sheaf of a `Spec k`-scheme
20	  `C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k))` viewed as a sheaf of `k`-modules. Built
21	  from the structure-morphism-derived ring map `k → Γ(C, U)` (helpers (1)–(5))
22	  and packaged as the presheaf `toModuleKPresheaf` (6), proven to be a sheaf
23	  by `toModuleKPresheaf_isSheaf` (7), and bundled as `toModuleKSheaf` (8).
24	
25	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_StructureSheafModuleK.tex`.
26	
27	## Status (iteration 006 — refactor scaffold)
28	
29	The two prerequisite declarations (lines below) are honestly closed (iter-005).
30	The eight Phase A step 5 main declarations are scaffolded as `sorry`. The
31	iter-006 prover round is responsible for filling them.
32	-/

34	set_option autoImplicit false

42	/-- Phase A step 5 prerequisite (a): sheafification on the topology of opens of
43	any topological space, valued in `ModuleCat k`. Inferable from Mathlib's
44	small-site / concrete-category sheafification API; the prover's task is the
45	universe pinning, mirroring the iter-004 `instHasSheafify_Opens_AddCommGrp`. -/
46	instance instHasSheafify_Opens_ModuleCatK
47	    (k : Type u) [CommRing k] (X : TopCat.{u}) :
48	    CategoryTheory.HasSheafify (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
49	      (ModuleCat.{u} k) :=
50	  inferInstance
51	
52	/-- Phase A step 5 prerequisite (b): `Ext` on the sheaf category. The universe
53	annotation `HasExt.{u+1}` is forced by the morphism universe of
54	`Sheaf (Opens.gT X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)`; mirrors the iter-004
55	`instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_AddCommGrp`. -/
56	noncomputable instance instHasExt_Sheaf_Opens_ModuleCatK
57	    (k : Type u) [CommRing k] (X : TopCat.{u}) :
58	    CategoryTheory.HasExt.{u+1}
59	      (CategoryTheory.Sheaf (Opens.grothendieckTopology X)
60	        (ModuleCat.{u} k)) :=

65	namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.toModuleKSheaf

67	variable {k : Type u} [CommRing k]
68	
69	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (1): the ring map `k → Γ(C, U)` induced by the
70	structure morphism `C.hom : C.left ⟶ Spec (CommRingCat.of k)` and the
71	restriction along `U ≤ ⊤`. -/
72	noncomputable def kToSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
73	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
74	    (CommRingCat.of k) ⟶ C.left.presheaf.obj U :=
75	  (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of k)).inv ≫
76	    C.hom.app ⊤ ≫ C.left.presheaf.map (homOfLE le_top).op
77	
78	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (2): `Γ(C, U)` is a `k`-algebra via the ring
79	map of `kToSection`. -/
80	noncomputable instance algebraSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
81	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
82	    Algebra k (C.left.presheaf.obj U) :=
83	  RingHom.toAlgebra (kToSection C U).hom
84	
85	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (3): unfolding lemma for the algebra map. -/
86	lemma algebraMap_eq_kToSection (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
87	    (U : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ) :
88	    (algebraMap k (C.left.presheaf.obj U)) = (kToSection C U).hom :=
89	  rfl
90	
91	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (4): the structure-morphism algebra map is
92	natural in `U`. -/
93	lemma kToSection_naturality (C : Over (Spec (CommRingCat.of k)))
94	    {U V : (TopologicalSpace.Opens C.left.toTopCat)ᵒᵖ} (f : U ⟶ V) :
95	    kToSection C U ≫ C.left.presheaf.map f = kToSection C V := by
96	  simp only [kToSection, Category.assoc, ← C.left.presheaf.map_comp]
97	  rfl
98	
99	/-- Phase A step 5 main, helper (5): corollary of (4) at the level of
100	`algebraMap`. -/

11	/-!
12	# Ext groups in abelian categories
13	
14	Let `C` be an abelian category (with `C : Type u` and `Category.{v} C`).
15	In this file, we introduce the assumption `HasExt.{w} C` which asserts
16	that morphisms between single complexes in arbitrary degrees in
17	the derived category of `C` are `w`-small. Under this assumption,
18	we define `Ext.{w} X Y n : Type w` as shrunk versions of suitable
19	types of morphisms in the derived category. In particular, when `C` has
20	enough projectives or enough injectives, the property `HasExt.{v} C`
21	shall hold.
22	
23	Note: in certain situations, `w := v` shall be the preferred
24	choice of universe (e.g. if `C := ModuleCat.{v} R` with `R : Type v`).
25	However, in the development of the API for Ext-groups, it is important
26	to keep a larger degree of generality for universes, as `w < v`
27	may happen in certain situations. Indeed, if `X : Scheme.{u}`,
28	then the underlying category of the étale site of `X` shall be a large
29	category. However, the category `Sheaf X.Etale AddCommGrpCat.{u}`
30	shall have good properties (because there is a small category of affine
31	schemes with the same category of sheaves), and even though the type of
32	morphisms in `Sheaf X.Etale AddCommGrpCat.{u}` shall be
33	in `Type (u + 1)`, these types are going to be `u`-small.
34	Then, for `C := Sheaf X.etale AddCommGrpCat.{u}`, we will have
35	`Category.{u + 1} C`, but `HasExt.{u} C` will hold
36	(as `C` has enough injectives). Then, the `Ext` groups between étale
37	sheaves over `X` shall be in `Type u`.
38	
39	-/
40	
41	@[expose] public section
42	
43	assert_not_exists TwoSidedIdeal
44	
45	universe w'' w' w v u
46	
47	namespace CategoryTheory
48	
49	variable (C : Type u) [Category.{v} C] [Abelian C]
50	
51	open Localization Limits ZeroObject DerivedCategory Pretriangulated
52	
53	/-- The property that morphisms between single complexes in arbitrary degrees are `w`-small
54	in the derived category. -/

533	section ChangeOfUniverse
534	
535	namespace Ext
536	
537	variable [HasExt.{w'} C] {X Y : C} {n : ℕ}
538	
539	/-- Up to an equivalence, the type `Ext.{w} X Y n` does not depend on the universe `w`. -/
540	noncomputable def chgUniv : Ext.{w} X Y n ≃ Ext.{w'} X Y n :=

### 1. **Can both `HasExt.{u}` and `HasExt.{u+1}` hold for the same category?**

    ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).precompOfLinear k F (zero_add n))

          α.inv_hom_id, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp])

-- u-flavor cover-totality: HModule' k F n T ≃ₗ[k] Ext.{u} (constantSheaf...) F n
-- Try with explicit .{u} on mk₀
noncomputable example :
    Scheme.HModule' k F n T
      ≃ₗ[k] Abelian.Ext.{u} ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n :=
  let α := HModule'_top_sourceIso k hT
  LinearEquiv.ofLinear
    ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀.{u} α.hom).precompOfLinear k F (zero_add n))
    ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀.{u} α.inv).precompOfLinear k F (zero_add n))
    (by

        ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀.{u} α.inv).comp x (zero_add n)) (zero_add n) = x
      rw [← Abelian.Ext.comp_assoc_of_second_deg_zero, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀,
          α.hom_inv_id, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp])
    (by

      change (Abelian.Ext.mk₀.{u} α.inv).comp

      rw [← Abelian.Ext.comp_assoc_of_second_deg_zero, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀,
          α.inv_hom_id, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp])
end

section
variable (k : Type u) [Field k]
variable {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
variable [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
variable [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
variable (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) (X : C)

noncomputable abbrev HModule'_v2 : Type (u+1) :=
  Abelian.Ext ((presheafToSheaf _ _).obj
    ((yoneda ⋙ (Functor.whiskeringRight _ _ _).obj (ModuleCat.free k)).obj X)) F n
end

end

import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.MayerVietoris

open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry Scheme

-- Approach: build HModule' k F n T ≃ₗ[k] HModule k F n directly using composition
-- without explicit chgUniv -- can we exploit that Lean's elaborator unifies?

section
variable (k : Type u) [Field k]
variable {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
variable [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
variable [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
variable (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T)

-- Try direct: use Ext.precompOfLinear with mk₀ α.inv from Ext.{u+1}
-- Maybe the iter-032 LinearEquiv can be applied via change of source object at u-level
noncomputable example : Scheme.HModule' k F n T ≃ₗ[k] Scheme.HModule k F n := by
  -- idea: HModule' k F n T = Ext.{u} (sheafified-yoneda T) F n
  -- iter-032 LinearEquiv: Ext.{u+1} (sheafified-yoneda T) F n ≃ₗ[k] HModule k F n
  -- Need: Ext.{u} (...) ≃ₗ[k] Ext.{u+1} (...) (same source/target)
  refine LinearEquiv.trans ?_ (Scheme.HModule_top_linearEquiv k F n hT)
  -- Goal: HModule' k F n T ≃ₗ[k] Ext.{u+1} (sheafified-yoneda T) F n
  sorry

end

variable {X Y : C} {n : ℕ}

example : Abelian.Ext.{0} X Y n ≃ Abelian.Ext.{1} X Y n :=
  Abelian.Ext.chgUniv

-- Find the AddEquiv structure
example (a b : Abelian.Ext.{0} X Y n) :
    Abelian.Ext.chgUniv.{1} (a + b) = Abelian.Ext.chgUniv a + Abelian.Ext.chgUniv b := by
  exact?

end

import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.MayerVietoris

open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry Scheme

-- Universe-u cover-totality Ext-transport: HModule' k F n T ≃ₗ[k] Ext.{u} (constantSheaf...) F n
-- Mirror of iter-032 but at universe u (i.e., on HModule' instead of HModule)

section
variable (k : Type u) [Field k]
variable {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
variable [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
variable [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
variable (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T)

-- Key: use Scheme.HModule' on the LHS to force universe u for Ext
noncomputable example :
    Scheme.HModule' k F n T
      ≃ₗ[k] Abelian.Ext ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n :=
  let α := HModule'_top_sourceIso k hT
  LinearEquiv.ofLinear
    ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).precompOfLinear k F (zero_add n))
    ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).precompOfLinear k F (zero_add n))
    (by
      ext x
      change (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).comp
        ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).comp x (zero_add n)) (zero_add n) = x
      rw [← Abelian.Ext.comp_assoc_of_second_deg_zero, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀,
          α.hom_inv_id, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp])
    (by
      ext y
      change (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).comp
        ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).comp y (zero_add n)) (zero_add n) = y
      rw [← Abelian.Ext.comp_assoc_of_second_deg_zero, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀,
          α.inv_hom_id, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp])
end

import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.MayerVietoris

open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry Scheme

section
variable (k : Type u) [Field k]
variable {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
variable [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
variable [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
variable (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T)

-- Universe-u cover-totality: HModule' k F n T ≃ₗ[k] Ext (constantSheaf...) F n
-- Note: argument order matches iter-032 — toFun via mk₀ α.inv, invFun via mk₀ α.hom
noncomputable example :
    Scheme.HModule' k F n T
      ≃ₗ[k] Abelian.Ext ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n :=
  let α := HModule'_top_sourceIso k hT
  LinearEquiv.ofLinear
    ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).precompOfLinear k F (zero_add n))
    ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).precompOfLinear k F (zero_add n))
    (by
      ext x
      change (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).comp
        ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).comp x (zero_add n)) (zero_add n) = x
      rw [← Abelian.Ext.comp_assoc_of_second_deg_zero, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀,
          α.inv_hom_id, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp])
    (by
      ext y
      change (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).comp
        ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).comp y (zero_add n)) (zero_add n) = y
      rw [← Abelian.Ext.comp_assoc_of_second_deg_zero, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀,
          α.hom_inv_id, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp])
end

import AlgebraicJacobian.Cohomology.MayerVietoris

open CategoryTheory Limits TopologicalSpace AlgebraicGeometry Scheme

namespace AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

noncomputable def myProbe
    (k : Type u) [Field k]
    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T) :
    HModule' k F n T
      ≃ₗ[k] Abelian.Ext ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n :=
  let α := HModule'_top_sourceIso k hT
  LinearEquiv.ofLinear
    ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).precompOfLinear k F (zero_add n))
    ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).precompOfLinear k F (zero_add n))
    (by
      ext x
      change (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).comp
        ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).comp x (zero_add n)) (zero_add n) = x
      rw [← Abelian.Ext.comp_assoc_of_second_deg_zero, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀,
          α.inv_hom_id, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp])
    (by
      ext y
      change (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).comp
        ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).comp y (zero_add n)) (zero_add n) = y
      rw [← Abelian.Ext.comp_assoc_of_second_deg_zero, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀,
          α.hom_inv_id, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp])

end AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme

3	## End-state
4	
5	`AlgebraicJacobian/Genus.lean`, `Jacobian.lean`, `AbelJacobi.lean` compile with **no `sorry`** and **no new `axiom`**, and the nine declarations frozen by `archon-protected.yaml` carry their intended mathematical content:
6	
7	- `genus C` = the genus of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve `C` over a field `k`.
8	- `Jacobian C : Over (Spec (.of k))` = the connected component of the identity in the Picard scheme of `C`, equipped with `GrpObj`, `IsProper`, `GeometricallyIrreducible`, and `SmoothOfRelativeDimension (genus C)`.
9	- `ofCurve P : C ⟶ Jacobian C` = the Abel–Jacobi map for a `k`-rational point `P`, sending `P` to `η[Jacobian C]`, and satisfying the Albanese universal property.
10	
11	## Honest assessment of feasibility (Mathlib b80f227, Lean 4.30.0-rc2)
12	
13	A full Mathlib survey (see `task_pending.md`) shows that **every single one of the nine protected declarations is blocked by non-trivial missing Mathlib infrastructure** — the gap is not in the proofs, it is in the surrounding theory. In particular:
14	
15	| Theory needed | Mathlib status |
16	|---|---|
17	| Sheaf cohomology of quasi-coherent sheaves on schemes (`Hⁱ(X, F)`) | absent (only `EllAdicCohomology` and abstract `RightDerived` machinery) |
18	| Sheaf of relative Kähler differentials `Ω_{X/S}` of schemes | absent (rings only) |
19	| Picard scheme / Picard functor / `Pic⁰` | absent (only `CommRing.Pic R`) |
20	| Hilbert polynomial of a coherent sheaf, arithmetic genus | absent |
21	| Riemann–Roch, Serre duality, rigidity for abelian varieties, Albanese | absent |
22	| Abelian variety / abelian scheme as a defined object | absent (encoded ad hoc via `[GrpObj] [IsProper] [GeometricallyIrreducible]`) |
23	
24	What **is** in Mathlib and we can lean on: `SmoothOfRelativeDimension`, `IsProper`, `GeometricallyIrreducible`, `Smooth`, `GrpObj` (with `instTensorUnit : GrpObj (𝟙_ C)`), `MonObj`, `η[·]`, the cartesian-monoidal structure on `Over (Spec (.of k))`, `Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/Group/Abelian.lean` (commutativity of proper geometrically integral group schemes — Stacks 0BFD), `KaehlerDifferential` for rings.
25	
26	This means **no honest local proof effort closes the protected declarations in this Mathlib**. We must not paper over the gap with ad-hoc definitions, weakened hypotheses, or new axioms (forbidden by the plan-agent rules). The path to completion is to **build the missing Mathlib chapter** ourselves inside this project, in the order forced by the dependency tree.
27

# Project Progress

## Current Stage
prover  (iteration 033 — Phase A step 6 *Path 2* / Serre-finiteness scaffolding **cover-totality Ext-transport at universe `u`**: append one declaration to `Cohomology/MayerVietoris.lean` inside the existing `section CoverTotality` (after iter-032's `HModule_top_linearEquiv`) — `noncomputable def Scheme.HModule'_top_linearEquiv` carrying a $k$-linear equivalence `HModule' k F n T ≃ₗ[k] Abelian.Ext ((constantSheaf J _).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n` for terminal `T`, with both sides at universe `u`. Plan-agent `lean_run_code` probe (this pass) confirms the body — `LinearEquiv.ofLinear ((mk₀ α.inv).precompOfLinear k F (zero_add n)) ((mk₀ α.hom).precompOfLinear k F (zero_add n))` with round-trip equations closed by the same four-step rewrite chain as iter-032 (`← Abelian.Ext.comp_assoc_of_second_deg_zero` + `Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀` + iso identity (`α.inv_hom_id` / `α.hom_inv_id`) + `Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp`) — typechecks end-to-end against the post-iter-032 file with `{success: true, diagnostics: []}` and kernel-only axioms `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`. The iter-032 prover's one-declaration close lands cleanly: 9 sorries (no change), zero diagnostics, kernel-only axioms, +48 LOC (720 → 768). Iter-033 sorry trajectory `9 → 9 → 9` (no transient scaffold sorries — body is a probe-confirmed term-mode one-liner with two `by`-block equations).

## Stages
- [x] init
- [x] autoformalize
- [ ] prover
- [ ] polish

## Big-picture context

This project formalizes the Jacobian of a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve over a field, following Christian Merten's challenge (`references/challenge.lean`). The nine declarations in `archon-protected.yaml` are the deliverables — their signatures are frozen.

`STRATEGY.md` describes the multi-phase build-out path (Phases A–E). Iter-005/006/007 closed Phase A steps 1–5 + polish. Iter-008 closed Track 2 (étale sheafification of `PicardFunctorAb`). Iter-009 closed the Phase A step 5/6 bridge (`HModule` infrastructure for `ModuleCat k`-valued sheaf cohomology). Iter-010 closed the Phase A step 6 *algebraic preliminary* (`HModule_zero_linearEquiv`). Iter-011 closed the **first protected sorry** (`AlgebraicGeometry.genus`). Iter-012 opened Path-2 Čech scaffold (`Scheme.cechCochain_OC`, `Scheme.cechCohomology_OC`). Iter-014/015 closed the Mayer-Vietoris prerequisite `Scheme.HModule'` and its `H⁰` bridge. Iter-016 → iter-026 closed the **complete Mayer-Vietoris LES** for the `ModuleCat k` flavor, mirroring Mathlib `MayerVietoris.lean` L43–149 line-by-line. Iter-027 split the cohort verbatim into a new file `Cohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`. Iter-028 opened **Serre-finiteness scaffolding**: a bundled 2-affine MV square `Scheme.AffineCoverMVSquare` together with the accessor `toMayerVietorisSquare`. Iter-029 appended four `@[simp]` corner-identification lemmas + a sheaf-parameterised LES specialisation `AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_sequence` + its exactness companion. Iter-030 appended the toModuleKSheaf-specialised LES on a curve `AffineCoverMVSquare.HModule'_sequence_curve` + exactness. Iter-031 appended the **cover-totality source-object iso** at the sheaf level — the natural iso identifying `(presheafToSheaf J _).obj ((yoneda ⋙ ModuleCat.free k).obj T)` with `(constantSheaf J _).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)` for terminal `T`. Iter-032 appended the **cover-totality Ext-transport at universe `u+1`** — the $k$-linear equivalence between `Abelian.Ext.{u+1}` of the sheafified-yoneda source and `HModule k F n` (the iter-009 protected target form). Iter-033 (this iteration) **appends the parallel cover-totality Ext-transport at universe `u`** — turning the iter-031 source iso into a $k$-linear equivalence between `HModule' k F n T` (the iter-014 form, at `Type u`) and `Ext ((constantSheaf J _).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n` (also at `Type u`). After iter-033, iter-034+ will build the **universe bump** from `Ext.{u}((constantSheaf J _).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n` (iter-033 RHS) to `Ext.{u+1}((constantSheaf J _).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n = HModule k F n` (iter-032 RHS), most plausibly via upgrading Mathlib's `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv : Ext.{w} X Y n ≃ Ext.{w'} X Y n` from a bare `Equiv` to a `LinearEquiv` (a Mathlib gap-fill); after that, the bridge `HModule' k F n T ≃ₗ[k] HModule k F n` follows by composition. Subsequently: specialise to `AffineCoverMVSquare`, attack the affine-vanishing `H^{>0}(Spec A, F) = 0` extraction, then the eventual Serre-finiteness `Module.Finite k (HModule k F i)` for $F = \toModuleKSheaf C$ on a proper $k$-curve, which `smoothOfRelativeDimension_genus` (Jacobian.lean) will eventually consume.

## Iteration 033 plan-agent summary (this iteration)

**Iter-032 verification.** Plan-agent verification (this pass) confirms iter-032 closure landed cleanly:

- **Sorry count**: `${LEAN4_PYTHON_BIN:-python3} "$LEAN4_SCRIPTS/sorry_analyzer.py" AlgebraicJacobian/ --format=summary` returns `9 total across 3 file(s)` (5 `Jacobian.lean` + 3 `AbelJacobi.lean` + 1 `Picard/Functor.lean`). Pre-iter-032 `9`, post-iter-032 `9`, target post-iter-033 prover `9`.
- **File diagnostics**: `lean_diagnostic_messages` on `AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/MayerVietoris.lean` returns `{success: true, items: [], failed_dependencies: []}`. Zero warnings, zero errors.
- **File LOC**: `Cohomology/MayerVietoris.lean` 768 LOC (iter-032 added 48 LOC, slightly above the +30 plan estimate due to the multi-paragraph load-bearing docstring). Still well under the ~800 LOC threshold; no further file split needed for iter-033.
- **`Genus.lean` preserved**: still 0 sorries; iter-011 first-protected closure intact.
- **`archon-protected.yaml`**: `git diff archon-protected.yaml` returns empty. Unchanged.
- **No new axioms**: no new `axiom` declarations in `AlgebraicJacobian/`.
- **Iter-032 axiom check**: `lean_verify AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule_top_linearEquiv` returns `{axioms: [propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound], warnings: [<pre-existing line 179 local instance, unrelated to iter-032>]}` — kernel-only (the warning is the harmless iter-019 `local instance` heuristic that has been present for many iterations).

**Iter-032 plan-agent forecast revisited.** The iter-032 plan-agent forecast iter-033 as "the universe bridge from `HModule' k F n T : Type u` to `Ext.{u+1}` (most plausibly via small refactor on iter-014 `HModule'` to drop its `Type u` ascription, or via `ULift.{u+1}` wrapper)". The iter-033 plan-agent re-probe (this pass) **revises** this assessment: the cleanest decomposition for iter-033 is **neither** a refactor of `HModule'` nor a `ULift` wrapper, but rather a **parallel cover-totality at universe `u`** (mirror of iter-032's universe-`u+1` Ext-transport, but at universe `u`). Three findings drove the revision:

1. **Refactor risk.** `grep -rn "HModule'" /home/archon/Lean_tests/AlgebraicJacobian/AlgebraicJacobian/ | wc -l` returns 171 mentions across two files (`StructureSheafModuleK.lean` and `Cohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`). Refactoring iter-014's `HModule'` to drop its `Type u` ascription would shift the cohort's `[HasExt _]` resolution from `HasExt.{u}` to `HasExt.{u+1}` everywhere. Risky cohort impact in a single iteration; deferred.

2. **`ULift.{u+1}` is awkward and requires Mathlib gap-fill.** The natural `Ext.{u} X Y n ≃ Ext.{u+1} X Y n` bridge is Mathlib's `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv` (file `Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Basic.lean` L540), but it is a bare `Equiv` — upgrading to a `LinearEquiv` requires manually proving additivity and `k`-scalar compatibility for the underlying `SmallShiftedHom.chgUniv`. This is a moderate-scope Mathlib gap-fill, separable from the cover-totality step.

3. **Step 3 splits cleanly into universe-`u+1` (iter-032) + universe-`u` (iter-033) + universe-bump (iter-034+).** Both `HasExt.{u}` and `HasExt.{u+1}` instances are auto-resolved for `Sheaf (Opens.gT X) (ModuleCat.{u} k)` (plan-agent probe confirmed via `inferInstance`). The cover-totality step at universe `u` is the universe-`u` analogue of iter-032: `HModule' k F n T ≃ₗ[k] Ext ((constantSheaf J _).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n`, both at `Type u`. The body is **identical in shape** to iter-032 — same `LinearEquiv.ofLinear`, same four-step rewrite chain — Lean's universe inference adapts automatically because the LHS `HModule' k F n T` forces `Ext.{u}` everywhere.

**Iter-033 decomposition decision.** Iter-033 closes **only the universe-`u` cover-totality Ext-transport step**, naming the result `HModule'_top_linearEquiv` (note the apostrophe: matches the `HModule'` LHS; the iter-032 declaration is `HModule_top_linearEquiv` without apostrophe, matching `HModule` on the RHS). The universe bump from `Ext.{u}((constantSheaf J _).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n` to `HModule k F n = Ext.{u+1}((constantSheaf J _).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n` is iter-034+ work. This is single-iteration in scope; lays clean groundwork for iter-034+ (one Mathlib gap-fill + one one-line composition); decouples cover-totality from universe alignment.

**Iter-033 Mathlib probe.** Plan-agent `lean_run_code` probe (this pass) confirms a one-declaration cohort compiles end-to-end against the post-iter-032 file with `{success: true, diagnostics: []}` and kernel-only axioms `[propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound]`:

- `noncomputable def AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.HModule'_top_linearEquiv` — natural $k$-linear equiv `HModule' k F n T ≃ₗ[k] Abelian.Ext ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n` for `[IsTerminal T]`. Body: `LinearEquiv.ofLinear ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).precompOfLinear k F (zero_add n)) ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).precompOfLinear k F (zero_add n)) <round-trip 1> <round-trip 2>` where `α := HModule'_top_sourceIso k hT` is the iter-031 source iso. Each round-trip closes via the same four-step rewrite chain as iter-032 — `← Abelian.Ext.comp_assoc_of_second_deg_zero` (associativity in the form `α.comp (β.comp γ) = (α.comp β).comp γ` since the middle factor `mk₀ α.hom` is degree 0), `Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀` (composition collapse `(mk₀ f).comp (mk₀ g) = mk₀ (f ≫ g)`), the iso identity (`α.inv_hom_id : α.inv ≫ α.hom = 𝟙 X₂` for round-trip 1; `α.hom_inv_id : α.hom ≫ α.inv = 𝟙 X₁` for round-trip 2), and `Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp` (identity-elimination `(mk₀ 𝟙).comp x = x`). Five Mathlib pieces composed verbatim: `Abelian.Ext.mk₀`, `Abelian.Ext.precompOfLinear`, `Abelian.Ext.comp_assoc_of_second_deg_zero`, `Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀`, `Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp` — same as iter-032, just at the `u`-flavour of `Ext`.

**Iter-033 work scope.** Append one new declaration to `Cohomology/MayerVietoris.lean` inside the **existing** `section CoverTotality` (after iter-032's `HModule_top_linearEquiv`, before `end CoverTotality`). Total expected source change: ~30 LOC append (one declaration + docstring). No protected files affected. Sorry count: `9 → 9` (no transient scaffold sorries — body is a probe-confirmed term-mode one-liner with two `by`-block equations).

**Refactor + prover sub-phase collapse acceptable.** The iter-033 prover may collapse refactor + prover into a single Edit (twenty-fifth consecutive prover round at this rate).

## Current Objectives

### 1. **`AlgebraicJacobian/Cohomology/MayerVietoris.lean`** — append one new declaration

Blueprint: `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_MayerVietoris.tex` § *Cover-totality Ext-transport at universe $u$ (iter-033)* (newly written this pass) — definition `def:Scheme_HModule_prime_top_linearEquiv`.

**Append one declaration inside the existing `section CoverTotality` at the end of the file** (after iter-032's `HModule_top_linearEquiv` declaration, before `end CoverTotality`). /-- Iter-033 cover-totality Ext-transport at universe `u`. The universe-`u`
parallel of iter-032's `HModule_top_linearEquiv`. Combining the iter-031
`HModule'_top_sourceIso` (a sheaf-level natural iso between source objects
of two `Ext` computations) with the Mathlib Ext-API operation
`precompOfLinear` (pre-composition of `Ext` along an `Ext 0`-element)
yields a `k`-linear equivalence between `HModule' k F n T` (the iter-014
universe-`u` cohomology of an open at the terminal) and the universe-`u`
`Ext` at the constant sheaf on `ModuleCat.of k k`.

Both sides live at `Type u` (matching iter-014's `HModule'` ascription).
This is the universe-`u` parallel of iter-032's universe-`u+1` form; the
final bridge between the two (`Ext.{u}((constantSheaf J _).obj
(ModuleCat.of k k)) F n ≃ₗ[k] HModule k F n = Ext.{u+1}(...)`) is
iter-034+ work, requiring Mathlib's `Abelian.Ext.chgUniv` (a bare
`Equiv`, file `Mathlib/Algebra/Homology/DerivedCategory/Ext/Basic.lean`
L540) to be upgraded to a `LinearEquiv`.

The body is shape-identical to iter-032: `LinearEquiv.ofLinear` with two
pre-composition maps via the iter-031 iso's hom/inv. The round-trip
equations close by the same four-step rewrite chain. Lean's universe
inference adapts automatically because the LHS `HModule' k F n T`
forces `Ext.{u}` everywhere. -/
noncomputable def HModule'_top_linearEquiv
    (k : Type u) [Field k]
    {C : Type v} [Category.{u, v} C] {J : GrothendieckTopology C}
    [HasWeakSheafify J (Type u)] [HasSheafify J (ModuleCat.{u} k)]
    [HasExt (Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k))]
    (F : Sheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)) (n : ℕ) {T : C} (hT : IsTerminal T) :
    HModule' k F n T
      ≃ₗ[k] Abelian.Ext ((constantSheaf J (ModuleCat.{u} k)).obj
              (ModuleCat.of k k)) F n :=
  let α := HModule'_top_sourceIso k hT
  LinearEquiv.ofLinear
    ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).precompOfLinear k F (zero_add n))
    ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).precompOfLinear k F (zero_add n))
    (by
      ext x
      change (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).comp
        ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).comp x (zero_add n)) (zero_add n) = x
      rw [← Abelian.Ext.comp_assoc_of_second_deg_zero, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀,
          α.inv_hom_id, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp])
    (by
      ext y
      change (Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.hom).comp
        ((Abelian.Ext.mk₀ α.inv).comp y (zero_add n)) (zero_add n) = y
      rw [← Abelian.Ext.comp_assoc_of_second_deg_zero, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_comp_mk₀,
          α.hom_inv_id, Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp])
